perturbation.original_text: 1.6 "Affiliate(s)" means, when used with respect to a specified Person, a Person that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with such specified Person. As used herein, "control" (including, with correlative meanings, "controlled by" and "under common control with"), when used with respect to any specified Person shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of such Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities or other interests, by contract, agreement, obligation, indenture, instrument, lease, promise, arrangement, release, warranty, commitment, undertaking or otherwise. It is expressly agreed that, from and after the Effective Date, solely for purposes of this Agreement (a) each member of the Spinco Group shall be deemed to not be an Affiliate of any member of the Pluto Group and (b) each member of the Pluto Group shall be deemed to not be an Affiliate of any member of the Spinco Group.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 "Affiliate(s)" means, when used with respect to a specified Person, a Person that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with such specified Person. As used herein, "control" (including, with correlative meanings, "controlled by" and "under common control with"), when used with respect to any specified Person shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of such Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities or other interests, by contract, agreement, obligation, indenture, instrument, lease, promise, arrangement, release, warranty, commitment, undertaking or otherwise. It is expressly agreed that, from and after the Effective Date, solely for purposes of this Agreement (a) each member of the Spinco Group **may be** deemed to not be an Affiliate of any member of the Pluto Group and (b) each member of the Pluto Group **may be** deemed to not be an Affiliate of any member of the Spinco Group.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "shall be deemed to not be" to "may be deemed to not be" in the definition of Affiliate introduces uncertainty. It directly contradicts the original intention of clearly separating the Spinco and Pluto Groups for the purposes of this agreement. Before it was expressly agreed that they were not affliates of each other, now with the change, it provides an opening for either party to change its position on whether they should be considered affiliates, the original express agreement, becomes uncertain. With this pertubation, there would be further confusion in the agreement, on clauses that refer to the definition of affiliate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 "Affiliate(s)" means, when used with respect to a specified Person, a Person that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with such specified Person. As used herein, "control" (including, with correlative meanings, "controlled by" and "under common control with"), when used with respect to any specified Person shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of such Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities or other interests, by contract, agreement, obligation, indenture, instrument, lease, promise, arrangement, release, warranty, commitment, undertaking or otherwise. It is expressly agreed that, from and after the Effective Date, solely for purposes of this Agreement (a) each member of the Spinco Group **may be** deemed to not be an Affiliate of any member of the Pluto Group and (b) each member of the Pluto Group **may be** deemed to not be an Affiliate of any member of the Spinco Group.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Spinco and Pluto groups 'shall be deemed to not be Affiliates' which creates a definitive separation. The changed text, however, uses 'may be deemed to not be Affiliates,' introducing uncertainty and the possibility of them being considered affiliates under certain conditions. This directly contradicts the original clear separation, creating an ambiguity about their affiliate status that would affect other sections of the agreement that rely on a clear definition of 'Affiliate'. This adheres to the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition, since it places a clause in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Forecasts and Purchase Orders. (a) VMR Products Forecasting and Purchase Orders. With respect to the VMR Products, the processes and mechanisms by which Forecasts are prepared and Purchase Orders are issued shall be as set forth in the applicable Facility Addenda and the remainder of this Section 2.4 shall not apply with respect to such VMR Products as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Forecasts and Purchase Orders. (a) VMR Products Forecasting and Purchase Orders. With respect to the VMR Products, the processes and mechanisms by which Forecasts are prepared and Purchase Orders are issued shall be as set forth in the applicable Facility Addenda and the remainder of this Section 2.4 shall **also** apply with respect to such VMR Products as applicable.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall not apply" to "shall also apply", Section 2.4 now contradict itself, it first states the forecasting of products is set forth in the appliccable facility addenda and that section 2.4 shall not apply with respect to such VMR products, but then it is saying it will also apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Forecasts and Purchase Orders. (a) VMR Products Forecasting and Purchase Orders. With respect to the VMR Products, the processes and mechanisms by which Forecasts are prepared and Purchase Orders are issued shall be as set forth in the applicable Facility Addenda and the remainder of this Section 2.4 shall **also** apply with respect to such VMR Products as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. Initially, Section 2.4(a) states that for VMR Products, the forecasting and purchase order processes are defined in the Facility Addenda, and the remainder of Section 2.4 *shall not apply*. The changed text alters this to *shall also apply*. This creates a clear conflict, as it simultaneously states that the remainder of Section 2.4 both applies and does not apply to VMR Products, making the contractual obligation regarding forecasting for VMR products ambiguous and unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 3.5 Invoices and Payment. Manufacturer shall submit invoices to Customer upon Delivery of Product. All invoices for Products will be in functional currency unless otherwise specified in the applicable Facility Addendum, and all undisputed payments hereunder shall be in full and be made without any withholding, offset or any other deductions.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.5 Invoices and Payment. Manufacturer shall submit invoices to Customer upon Delivery of Product. All invoices for Products will be in functional currency unless otherwise specified in the applicable Facility Addendum, and all undisputed payments hereunder **may not** be in full and be made without any withholding, offset or any other deductions.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting "may not" payments are now no longer guaranteed to be made in full, it contradicts the original statement which states the payments will be full with no deductions, withholdings, or offsets. This introduces uncertainty about the financial obligations of Customer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.5 Invoices and Payment. Manufacturer shall submit invoices to Customer upon Delivery of Product. All invoices for Products will be in functional currency unless otherwise specified in the applicable Facility Addendum, and all undisputed payments hereunder **may not** be in full and be made without any withholding, offset or any other deductions.
perturbation.justification: The original text states payments 'shall be in full', creating a clear obligation. The changed text introduces 'may not be in full', directly contradicting this obligation. This introduces uncertainty about the financial obligations of Customer. Therefore a strong contradiction exists.
